[File: 'File Name: src/SABLE/CommunityIssuance.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: issueSABLE / _updateG] Can an unprivileged depositor under the precondition that totalUSDSDeposits == 0 at the time issueSABLE() is called trigger the call sequence (1) wait T seconds with no SP depositors, (2) call provideToSP() which calls _triggerSABLEIssuance() -> issueSABLE() returning latestRewardPerSec * T, (3) _updateG() skips G update because totalUSDS == 0, (4) totalSABLEIssued increases by latestRewardPerSec * T but epochToScaleToG is unchanged, (5) attacker is now the sole depositor with G snapshot at current G, (6) future issuance accrues entirely to attacker, violating the invariant that SABLE issued during zero-deposit periods must not be claimable by any later depositor and that the CommunityIssuance SABLE balance must remain consistent with totalSABLEIssued, causing scoped impact: attacker captures 100% of all future SABLE issuance as sole depositor while SABLE tokens corresponding to the zero-deposit gap remain stranded in CommunityIssuance and are never distributed? Local proof idea: write a Foundry unit test that deploys the system, leaves SP empty for 1000 seconds, then has attacker call provideToSP(1e18), wait another 1000 seconds, call withdrawFromSP(0) to claim SABLE gains, and assert that attacker's SABLE gain equals only the second 1000-second window's issuance, not the first.

[File: 'File Name: src/SABLE/CommunityIssuance.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: issueSABLE] Can an unprivileged depositor under the precondition that the CommunityIssuance contract holds fewer SABLE tokens than totalSABLEIssued would imply trigger the call sequence (1) issueSABLE() is called, computing issuance = latestRewardPerSec * timeSinceLastIssue and adding to totalSABLEIssued without checking sableToken.balanceOf(address(this)), (2) _updateG() updates epochToScaleToG with the phantom issuance, (3) depositor calls withdrawFromSP() which calls _payOutSABLEGains() -> communityIssuance.sendSABLE() -> sableToken.transfer() which reverts because balance is insufficient, violating the invariant that every G accumulator increment must correspond to SABLE tokens actually held by CommunityIssuance, causing scoped impact: all SP withdrawals and deposits are permanently bricked because sendSABLE always reverts, and depositors cannot claim any SABLE rewards even for legitimately earned amounts? [File: 'File Name: src/SABLE/CommunityIssuance.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: updateRewardPerSec / issueSABLE] Can an unprivileged depositor under the precondition that the owner is about to call updateRewardPerSec() to increase latestRewardPerSec trigger the call sequence (1) attacker front-runs updateRewardPerSec() by calling provideToSP() in the same block, which calls issueSABLE() setting lastIssuanceTime = block.timestamp and updating G with the old rate, (2) owner's updateRewardPerSec() calls stabilityPool.ownerTriggerIssuance() which calls issueSABLE() again in the same block, issuing 0 SABLE (timeSinceLastIssue = 0), (3) require(lastIssuanceTime == block.timestamp) passes, (4) latestRewardPerSec is set to the new higher value, (5) attacker's snapshot G is now at the post-rate-change G baseline, so attacker earns the new higher rate from the next block, violating the invariant that rate changes must not allow any depositor to gain an unfair timing advantage over others with equal deposits, causing scoped impact: attacker captures disproportionate SABLE rewards by entering the pool at the exact block of a rate increase, earning the higher rate from block 0 while other depositors who entered earlier have their snapshots at a lower G? Local proof idea: Foundry fuzz test with two depositors of equal size, one entering one block before rate increase and one entering at the rate-increase block, assert their per-unit SABLE gains are equal over the same subsequent time window.

[File: 'File Name: src/SABLE/CommunityIssuance.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: issueSABLE / constructor] Can an unprivileged depositor under the precondition that setParams() is called significantly later than the constructor (deploymentTime and lastIssuanceTime are both set to constructor block.timestamp) trigger the call sequence (1) constructor sets lastIssuanceTime = block.timestamp at T0, (2) setParams() is called at T1 >> T0, (3) first call to issueSABLE() at T1 computes timeSinceLastIssue = T1 - T0, issuing latestRewardPerSec * (T1 - T0) as a lump sum, (4) if totalUSDSDeposits > 0 at T1, G is updated with this entire lump sum, (5) the depositor who entered between T0 and T1 (impossible since SP wasn't set up) or the first depositor at T1 captures this lump sum, violating the invariant that SABLE issuance must only accrue to depositors who were present during the issuance period, causing scoped impact: the first depositor after setParams captures a large lump-sum SABLE reward corresponding to the entire deployment gap, extracting SABLE that should not have been issued? Local proof idea: unit test deploying CommunityIssuance, waiting 30 days before calling setParams, then having a single depositor call provideToSP and assert their SABLE gain equals 30 days * latestRewardPerSec.

[File: 'File Name: src/SABLE/CommunityIssuance.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: sendSABLE / _payOutSABLEGains] Can an unprivileged front-end operator under the precondition that they registered with kickbackRate = 0 and have depositors tagged to them trigger the call sequence (1) front-end registers with kickbackRate = 0 (depositors get 0% of SABLE, front-end gets 100%), (2) depositors call provideToSP() tagging this front-end, (3) on any SP interaction, _payOutSABLEGains() calls getFrontEndSABLEGain() which computes frontEndShare = DECIMAL_PRECISION - 0 = DECIMAL_PRECISION (100%), (4) communityIssuance.sendSABLE(frontEnd, frontEndSABLEGain) sends 100% of the deposit's SABLE to the front-end, (5) getDepositorSABLEGain() returns kickbackRate * gain / DECIMAL_PRECISION = 0, (6) depositor receives 0 SABLE, violating the invariant that depositors must receive SABLE proportional to their deposit share regardless of front-end configuration, causing scoped impact: front-end operator extracts 100% of all SABLE rewards from tagged depositors who may not understand the kickbackRate = 0 configuration? Local proof idea: unit test registering a front-end with kickbackRate = 0, having a depositor tag it, waiting for issuance, withdrawing, and asserting depositor SABLE gain = 0 while front-end received the full amount.

[File: 'File Name: src/SABLE/CommunityIssuance.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: issueSABLE / _computeSABLEPerUnitStaked] Can an unprivileged depositor under the precondition that totalUSDSDeposits is very small (e.g., 1 wei) trigger the call sequence (1) attacker deposits 1 wei USDS into SP, (2) issueSABLE() returns a large issuance amount, (3) _computeSABLEPerUnitStaked computes SABLENumerator = issuance * 1e18 + lastSABLEError, SABLEPerUnitStaked = SABLENumerator / 1 = SABLENumerator (potentially overflowing uint256 or producing an astronomically large value), (4) marginalSABLEGain = SABLEPerUnitStaked * P overflows or produces a value far exceeding CommunityIssuance's SABLE balance, (5) G is updated with this inflated value, (6) attacker's SABLE gain calculation returns a value larger than CommunityIssuance holds, violating the invariant that total claimable SABLE gains must never exceed CommunityIssuance's actual SABLE balance, causing scoped impact: attacker with a dust deposit extracts all SABLE from CommunityIssuance or causes arithmetic overflow that bricks the SP? [File: 'File Name: src/SABLE/CommunityIssuance.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: updateRewardPerSec] Can an unprivileged depositor under the precondition that the owner sets latestRewardPerSec to type(uint256).max or a value that causes overflow trigger the call sequence (1) owner calls updateRewardPerSec(type(uint256).max), (2) next call to issueSABLE() computes latestRewardPerSec.mul(timeSinceLastIssue) which overflows SafeMath and reverts, (3) every SP operation (provideToSP, withdrawFromSP, offset, withdrawBNBGainToTrove) calls _triggerSABLEIssuance() which calls issueSABLE() and reverts, (4) all SP operations are permanently bricked, violating the invariant that no parameter setting must permanently prevent depositors from withdrawing their USDS and BNB gains, causing scoped impact: all depositors are permanently locked out of their USDS deposits and BNB gains because every SP entry point reverts? [File: 'File Name: src/SABLE/CommunityIssuance.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: _payOutSABLEGains / sendSABLE] Can an unprivileged depositor under the precondition that they have a non-zero deposit and their front-end address is non-zero trigger the call sequence (1) _payOutSABLEGains() first calls communityIssuance.sendSABLE(frontEnd, frontEndSABLEGain), (2) then calls communityIssuance.sendSABLE(depositor, depositorSABLEGain), (3) if the front-end is a malicious contract that reverts on SABLE receipt, the entire _payOutSABLEGains() reverts, (4) the depositor's SABLE gains are permanently locked because every SP interaction that calls _payOutSABLEGains() will revert, violating the invariant that a front-end's behavior must not prevent depositors from claiming their own SABLE gains, causing scoped impact: a malicious front-end operator can permanently lock all tagged depositors' SABLE gains by making their contract revert on SABLE transfer? [File: 'File Name: src/SABLE/CommunityIssuance.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: issueSABLE / _updateG / epochToScaleToG] Can an unprivileged depositor under the precondition that the SP has undergone a scale change (currentScale incremented) trigger the call sequence (1) depositor A deposits at scale 0, (2) liquidations cause scale to increment to 1, (3) issueSABLE() is called, _updateG() updates epochToScaleToG[epoch][1], (4) depositor A's _getSABLEGainFromSnapshots computes firstPortion = epochToScaleToG[epoch][0] - G_snapshot (no new G at scale 0), secondPortion = epochToScaleToG[epoch][1] / SCALE_FACTOR (divided by 1e9), (5) depositor B deposits at scale 1 with G snapshot at epochToScaleToG[epoch][1], (6) depositor B's firstPortion = epochToScaleToG[epoch][1] - G_snapshot_B (full amount), (7) depositor B receives full G[1] while depositor A receives G[1]/1e9, violating the invariant that depositors with equal deposits over the same time window must receive equal SABLE gains, causing scoped impact: depositor B extracts disproportionately more SABLE than depositor A with the same deposit size by entering after a scale change? [File: 'File Name: src/SABLE/CommunityIssuance.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: sendSABLE / _requireCallerIsStabilityPool] Can an unprivileged attacker under the precondition that they can deploy a contract that passes the checkContract() check and is set as the stabilityPool address trigger the call sequence (1) if setParams() is called with a malicious contract address that passes checkContract() (has code), (2) the malicious contract calls communityIssuance.sendSABLE(attacker, communityIssuance.balanceSABLE()) directly, (3) all SABLE held by CommunityIssuance is transferred to the attacker, violating the invariant that sendSABLE must only be callable by the legitimate StabilityPool contract, causing scoped impact: attacker drains all SABLE from CommunityIssuance by impersonating the StabilityPool? [File: 'File Name: src/SABLE/CommunityIssuance.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: issueSABLE / _updateG] Can an unprivileged depositor under the precondition that they deposit a very large amount relative to totalUSDSDeposits trigger the call sequence (1) totalUSDSDeposits = 1e18, (2) attacker deposits 1e36 USDS (if they have it), making their share ~100% of the pool, (3) issueSABLE() issues R*dt, _computeSABLEPerUnitStaked computes SABLEPerUnitStaked = issuance * 1e18 / (1e18 + 1e36) ≈ 0 due to integer division, (4) G is updated with marginalSABLEGain = 0 * P = 0, (5) no SABLE is distributed despite issuance being non-zero, (6) SABLE stays in CommunityIssuance, violating the invariant that all issued SABLE must be distributed to depositors proportionally, causing scoped impact: a whale depositor causes SABLE issuance to be silently lost due to integer division truncation when their deposit dwarfs the issuance amount? [File: 'File Name: src/SABLE/CommunityIssuance.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: updateRewardPerSec / ownerTriggerIssuance] Can an unprivileged depositor under the precondition that updateRewardPerSec() is called and the require(lastIssuanceTime == block.timestamp) check can be bypassed trigger the call sequence (1) owner calls updateRewardPerSec(newRate), (2) stabilityPool.ownerTriggerIssuance() calls _triggerSABLEIssuance() -> issueSABLE() which sets lastIssuanceTime = block.timestamp, (3) require(lastIssuanceTime == block.timestamp) passes trivially since issueSABLE() just set it, (4) if a miner manipulates block.timestamp to be different from what issueSABLE() recorded... but since they're in the same transaction, block.timestamp is the same, (5) however if ownerTriggerIssuance() reverts for any reason (e.g., SP is paused), the require check is never reached and updateRewardPerSec() reverts, (6) owner cannot update the rate, violating the invariant that rate updates must always be possible when the owner calls them, causing scoped impact: if ownerTriggerIssuance() is blocked, the rate can never be updated, locking the reward rate permanently?
